summary.intent: Verify git-ai v1.3.1 installation, systematically test all its commands and data access patterns, and produce a reference document comparing git-ai's capabilities against the existing BMAD checkpoint system.
summary.outcome: Installation was verified and a test commit was made to src/lib/types.ts to generate attribution data. The session was cut off before the full command testing, schema extraction, gap analysis, and document creation were completed.

repo: The repo at /Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics uses a custom BMAD checkpoint system on a separate GitHub branch (entire/checkpoints/v1 in ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad) to track AI metrics.
git-ai hooks are configured in ~/.claude/settings.json as PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks running: git-ai checkpoint claude --hook-input stdin
git-ai stores data in two SQLite databases: a prompts DB and an internal DB (~/.git-ai/internal/db), both were empty at session start (fresh install).
git-ai uses git notes under refs/notes/ai to store per-commit attribution data; no notes existed before the test commit.

code.finding: File was edited with a small test change to generate the first git-ai attribution data after fresh install.

workflow: Fresh git-ai install produces empty SQLite tables and no git notes until the first tool-call-instrumented commit is made.
The session itself (with hooks firing on every tool call) serves as the live data source for git-ai attribution testing.
Token counts (input/cache_creation/cache_read/output) come from Claude API response metadata and are not tracked by git-ai, which is the key gap vs the BMAD CheckpointMeta type.

friction: Session was incomplete — the transcript ends mid-task after making the test commit, before running any of the data query commands (stats, status, log, show, diff, blame, search, prompts SQL, raw git notes).
Databases were empty at start (fresh install), requiring a test commit before any meaningful data could be queried.
open_items: Run all data access commands (git-ai stats, status, log, show, diff, blame, search, prompts exec SQL) and capture full JSON output.
Document complete SQLite schema for both the prompts DB and internal DB.
Document raw git notes structure written per commit after the test commit.
Perform field-by-field gap analysis comparing git-ai output to CheckpointMeta fields (agent_percentage, agent_lines, human_added/modified/removed, tokens, files_touched, turns, bmad_commands).
Write /Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/docs/git-ai-reference.md with all findings and integration recommendation.
